description: A sub-interface is not supported as an underlay for EVPN-VXLAN.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX release 10.13.0005, what is the caveat related to Sub-Interface?
Feature Caveat: Sub-Interface - A sub-interface is not supported as an underlay for EVPN-VXLAN.

description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what is the caveat related to SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. If you create a traffic stream between connected hosts and enable the ECN bit for the hosts and start the traffic, a traffic capture may show that ECN bits arent copied from the inner header to the outer header.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?

Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. If you create a traffic stream between connected hosts and enable the ECN bit for the hosts and start the traffic, a traffic capture may show that ECN bits are not copied from the inner header to the outer header.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what is the caveat regarding REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

description: In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer.

description: Symptom: The VPNv4 neighbor is in idle state and triggers a Cease/Connection Rejected error code. Scenario: In an MPLS L3VPN with six VRFs in two sites, after deleting the loopback on PE2 and reading the configuration using a checkpoint, the VPNv4 session stays in an idle state and a Cease/Connection Rejected error code is sent. Workaround: Restart the hpe-routing daemon.

description: Symptom: VXLAN tunnels go down after removing interfaces with IPv6 address that are the same as the VXLAN VTEP IP addresses. Scenario: In an EVPN-VXLAN deployment with an IPv6 tunnel, if any interface (irrespective of the VRF) that has same IP address as the tunnel source IP, it goes down, and then the tunnel interface is brought down. Workaround: Unconfigure loopback and VXLAN and re-configure them.

description: Symptom: After uploading an invalid PEM certificate, the hpe-restd process becomes unstable, the WebUI temporarily stops responding, and all REST API calls from the WebUI fail. Scenario: If a user uploads a corrupt PEM certificate file using WebUI certificate management window, selecting the Upload button in the WebUI causes the WebUI to stop working completely. To recover from this state, restart hpe-restd from the bash prompt in the command-line interface or restart the switch. Workaround: Use the CLI to upload certificates.

description: Symptom: Clients are onboarding with an incorrect auth-priority order. Users onboarded with a lower-priority authentication method, so higher-priority authentication requests were not seen. Scenario: When concurrent onboarding is enabled with a default authentication priority and non-default auth-precedence on a port, clients onboarding are taking the wrong auth-priority. Workaround: At the issue state, reconfigure the auth-priority on the port to onboard the clients.

description: Symptom: ERSPAN/Mirror Endpoint interoperability issues occur between platforms with differing ASIC manufacturers. Scenario: There is an established tunnel session between two switches, along with an ERSPAN session using that tunnel. However, the Mirroring Endpoint on the 6xxx/8360 device is not decapsulating the packets. The Mirroring Endpoint expects packets with protocol type 0x6558 for ERSPAN, but the transmitting device is using protocol type 0x88BE. As a result, no packets are being recognized by the Mirror Endpoint. Workaround: Use a SPAN session with the destination of a directly attached device rather than ERSPAN.

description: Symptom: The NTP conductor won't be available to serve NTP clients on all interfaces for the VRF with both a client and conductor; instead, it will only listen on the configured source interface. Scenario: When the client and conductor are on the same VRF and the NTP source interface is configured for that VRF, it will affect which interface the NTP conductor listens on. Workaround: Maintain the conductor on a separate VRF.

description: Symptom: IPv6 Prefixes with a subnet mask between /65 to /127 will not get programmed in ASIC. Scenario: In regular routing scenarios, packets will be software forwarded. In a VxLAN overlay scenario, the packets will be dropped. It is recommended to use a /128 prefix for a loopback used as an IPv6 VXLAN tunnel source.

description: Symptom: Failure to establish or maintain a connection to Aruba Central. Scenario: A switch may fail to establish or maintain its relationship with Aruba Central. In these cases, the switch may display the "Central source connection status" field in the "show aruba-central" command will read "connection_failure". Workaround: Issue the following commands in order: start-shell; sudo bash; systemctl restart_jitterentropy-rngd; exit; exit; show aruba-central.

description: Symptom: A route takes 180 seconds to get completely learned and stabilized when the feature enabled using the command route recursive default route is disabled during the route flap. Scenario: A route flap is observed upon any uplink failover scenarios when the nexthop of the BGP route tries to resolve via default route. In order to avoid the flap, route recursive default route ipv4/ipv6 should be disabled. if the cli is disabled during the issue state. Route learning takes 180 seconds for learning it completely. Cli "Route recursive default route ipv4/ipv6" needs to be disabled first. Workaround: clear bgp \*

description: Symptom: When DHCP Snooping and DHCP Relay are enabled together on VTEPS, the DHCP Server will receive duplicate DHCP packets. Scenario: By default, VxLAN tunnel interfaces are trusted. To avoid duplicate packets on DHCP Server when DHCP Snooping and DHCP Relay are enabled together on VTEPs, should disable trust on VxLAN tunnel interfaces. These configurations can be enabled using the new no dhcpv4-snooping tunnel vxlan trust option for DHCPv4 Snooping and the new no dhcpv6-snooping tunnel vxlan trust option for DHCPv6 Snooping.

description: Symptom: The VPNv4 neighbor is in idle state with a Cease/Connection Rejected error code. Scenario: In an MPLS L3VPN with six VRFs in two sites learning a total of 40K from each site, after deleting the loopback on PE2 and reading the configuration using checkpoint, the VPNv4 session stays in an idle state and a Cease/Connection Rejected error code is sent. Workaround: Restart the hpe-routing daemon.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

description: Symptom: The VPNv4 neighbor is in idle state and triggers a Cease/Connection Rejected error code. Scenario: In an MPLS L3VPN with six VRFs in two sites, after deleting the loopback on PE2 and reading the configuration using a checkpoint, the VPNv4 session stays in an idle state and a Cease/Connection Rejected error code is sent. Workaround: Restart the hpe-routing daemon.

description: Symptom: VXLAN tunnels go down after removing interfaces with IPv6 address that are the same as the VXLAN VTEP IP addresses. Scenario: In an EVPN-VXLAN deployment with an IPv6 tunnel, if any interface (irrespective of the VRF) that has same IP address as the tunnel source IP, it goes down, and then the tunnel interface is brought down. Workaround: Unconfigure loopback and VXLAN and re-configure them.

description: Symptom: Clients are onboarding with an incorrect auth-priority order. Users onboarded with a lower-priority authentication method, so higher-priority authentication requests were not seen. Scenario: When concurrent onboarding is enabled with a default authentication priority and non-default auth-precedence on a port, clients onboarding are taking the wrong auth-priority. Workaround: At the issue state, reconfigure the auth-priority on the port to onboard the clients.

description: Symptom: ERSPAN/Mirror Endpoint interoperability issues occur between platforms with differing ASIC manufacturers. Scenario: There is an established tunnel session between two switches, along with an ERSPAN session using that tunnel. However, the Mirroring Endpoint on the 6xxx/8360 device is not decapsulating the packets. The Mirroring Endpoint expects packets with protocol type 0x6558 for ERSPAN, but the transmitting device is using protocol type 0x88BE. As a result, no packets are being recognized by the Mirror Endpoint. Workaround: Use a SPAN session with the destination of a directly attached device rather than ERSPAN.

description: Symptom: The NTP conductor won't be available to serve NTP clients on all interfaces for the VRF with both a client and conductor; instead, it will only listen on the configured source interface. Workaround: Maintain the conductor on a separate VRF.

description: Symptom: IPv6 Prefixes with a subnet mask between /65 to /127 will not get programmed in ASIC. Scenario: In regular routing scenarios, packets will be software forwarded. In a VxLAN overlay scenario, the packets will be dropped. It is recommended to use a /128 prefix for a loopback used as an IPv6 VXLAN tunnel source.

description: Symptom: Failure to establish or maintain a connection to Aruba Central. Scenario: A switch may fail to establish or maintain its relationship with Aruba Central. In these cases, the switch may display the "Central source connection status" field in the "show aruba-central" command will read "connection_failure". Workaround: Issue the following commands in order: start-shell; sudo bash; systemctl restart_jitterentropy-rngd; exit; exit; show aruba-central.

description: Symptom: A route takes 180 seconds to get completely learned and stabilized when the feature enabled using the command route recursive default route is disabled during the route flap. Scenario: A route flap is observed upon any uplink failover scenarios when the nexthop of the BGP route tries to resolve via default route. In order to avoid the flap, route recursive default route ipv4/ipv6 should be disabled. if the cli is disabled during the issue state. Route learning takes 180 seconds for learning it completely. Cli "Route recursive default route ipv4/ipv6" needs to be disabled first. Workaround: clear bgp \*

description: Symptom: When DHCP Snooping and DHCP Relay are enabled together on VTEPS, the DHCP Server will receive duplicate DHCP packets. Scenario: By default, VxLAN tunnel interfaces are trusted. To avoid duplicate packets on DHCP Server when DHCP Snooping and DHCP Relay are enabled together on VTEPs, should disable trust on VxLAN tunnel interfaces. These configurations can be enabled using the new no dhcpv4-snooping tunnel vxlan trust option for DHCPv4 Snooping and the new no dhcpv6-snooping tunnel vxlan trust option for DHCPv6 Snooping.

These configurations can be enabled using the new no dhcpv4-snooping tunnel vxlan trust option for DHCPv4 Snooping and the new no dhcpv6-snooping tunnel vxlan trust option for DHCPv6 Snooping.

DHCP Relay (Bug ID 240054): No workaround is documented in the release notes.

description: Symptom: The VPNv4 neighbor is in idle state with a Cease/Connection Rejected error code. Scenario: In an MPLS L3VPN with six VRFs in two sites learning a total of 40K from each site, after deleting the loopback on PE2 and reading the configuration using checkpoint, the VPNv4 session stays in an idle state and a Cease/Connection Rejected error code is sent. Workaround: Restart the hpe-routing daemon.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

description: Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1
